close_reason: Completed: jsdeps-gen tool, js-deps-cell.nix, JavaScript adapter, turnkey module integration
description:
Implement pnpm dependency management following the same pattern as Go, Rust, and Python:

## Why pnpm
- Stricter dependency resolution (no phantom dependencies)
- pnpm-lock.yaml is well-structured and deterministic
- Used by rules_js (Aspect) - proven approach for Buck2/Bazel
- Better suited for monorepo setups

## Goals
1. Take pnpm-lock.yaml as input
2. Convert it to a list of artifacts to fetch with hashes
3. Use Nix to fetch npm tarballs with proper caching
4. Augment packages with BUCK files for Buck2 builds
5. Organize into a `jsdeps` cell

## Required Components

### jsdeps-gen tool
- Parse pnpm-lock.yaml
- Generate TOML with package info and hashes
- Similar to godeps-gen, rustdeps-gen, pydeps-gen

### Nix derivations
- Fetch npm tarballs from registry
- Extract and organize packages
- Generate BUCK files for each package

### Buck2 rules
- `npm_library` or `js_library` rule for npm packages
- Integration with `typescript_library` for imports

### Cell structure
```
jsdeps//
├── BUCK
├── lodash/
│   ├── BUCK
│   └── ... (package contents)
├── react/
│   ├── BUCK
│   └── ...
```

## Prior Art
- rules_js uses pnpm and its lockfile
- pnpm2nix for Nix integration

## Example Usage
```bash
# Generate deps file from lockfile
jsdeps-gen --lock pnpm-lock.yaml -o js-deps.toml

# Use in TypeScript library
typescript_library(
    name = "app",
    srcs = ["app.ts"],
    deps = ["jsdeps//:lodash"],
)
```
title: Implement pnpm dependency management for TypeScript
